ksa_text: Knowledge of AI model supply chain security and risk management, including third-party component risks, open-source model adoption risks, fine-tuning pipeline integrity, dataset provenance verification, and model registry controls.

dcwf.top_element_label: Ensure security design and cybersecurity development activities are properly documented (providing a functional description of security implementation) and updated as necessary.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply chain issues.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply chain issues.
Ensure security design and cybersecurity development activities are properly documented (providing a functional description of security implementation) and updated as necessary.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: This knowledge KSA describes hands-on technical execution that falls below the expected seniority threshold for a VP Data Science (vp tier). Execution-level work in Security & Platform Strategy is not expected at this level.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.challenge: Seniority fit was marked 'too_junior', but the dimension is Knowledge — awareness-level KSAs are typically appropriate across seniority tiers.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.rebuttal: Even Knowledge-level KSAs that describe specific low-level technical details (e.g., implementation internals) are not expected at the vp level. Exclusion upheld.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: Exclusion upheld: Knowledge KSAs with predominantly execution-oriented framing are appropriately excluded at the vp tier.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could real-world VP Data Science practitioners be expected to demonstrate this knowledge KSA in practice, despite the seniority gap?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: NewVantage Partners CDO Survey, Wavestone CDAIO Report, and Gartner leadership effectiveness research confirm that vp-tier roles delegate execution responsibilities to appropriate levels rather than owning them directly.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: seniority-based exclusion is consistent with external evidence on vp-tier role expectations. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Seniority mismatch: this knowledge KSA is below the expected level for the VP Data Science (vp tier). Consistently excluded.
